### Post by ZiVvmO on 2007-08-06
i installed ntfs-3g yesterday and everything has worked great, until i just rebooted and not my windows drive is not mounted anymore.  its in my fstab....but looking in konqueror, the folder its normally mounted to is empty.

any ideas?

my fstab:

> # /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda7 :
UUID=997f35d5-88ca-413d-8abd-f39d49c295b7 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=79a043a0-9499-4143-aba3-4df110dba8b1 /boot ext3 defaults 0 2
# Entry for /dev/sda5 :
UUID=C433-E80F /btw vfat defaults,utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=6C94A75B94A72714 /windows ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda6 :
UUID=42657ce7-b9af-42a1-a789-3a11fd6faca1 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0

pre ntfs-3g fstab:

> # /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>
proc            /proc           proc    defaults        0       0
# /dev/sda7
UUID=997f35d5-88ca-413d-8abd-f39d49c295b7 /               ext3    defaults,errors=remount-ro 0       1
# /dev/sda2
UUID=79a043a0-9499-4143-aba3-4df110dba8b1 /boot           ext3    defaults        0       2
# /dev/sda5
UUID=C433-E80F  /btw            vfat    defaults,utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0       1
# /dev/sda1
UUID=6C94A75B94A72714 /windows        ntfs    defaults,nls=utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0       1
# /dev/sda6
UUID=42657ce7-b9af-42a1-a789-3a11fd6faca1 none            swap    sw              0       0
/dev/scd0       /media/cdrom0   udf,iso9660 user,noauto     0       0

### Post by nitro_n2o on 2007-08-06
well.. that sounds good to me 
UUID=6C94A75B94A72714 /windows ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
it's mounted in the root directory i guess

### Post by ZiVvmO on 2007-08-06
well it should be mounted in the /windows directory, but its not.

the strange thing is that it worked after i installed it yesterday and all day today, until just now.  i dont know if it's coincidence that i booted in windows earlier and it stopped working after going into windows, but....eh?

---

### Post by merlinus on 2007-08-06
Did you exit windows normally?

ubuntu will not mount drives that it detects has errors.

Try running chkdsk c with the /f switch from windows.  You will have to reboot and it will then run.

-merlin

---

### Post by ZiVvmO on 2007-08-06
i hibernated windows actually.  that could be it?  however, ive been hibernating all the time and its always worked until ntfs-3g.

---

### Post by merlinus on 2007-08-06
You got it.  ntfs-3g allow writing to ntfs partitions, which cannot be done in hibernation.

You pays your money and picks your poison.

-merlin

---

### Post by ZiVvmO on 2007-08-06
well that makes sense, cuz windows could get really confused if it hibernated and everything was in a different place when it turned spring again.

i would be.

---

### Post by ZiVvmO on 2007-08-06
yep.  i restarted windows and shut it down and now the mount works again.

thanks for the help.

---
